LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT HANIF AMERZADA'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGEMENT ON HANIF AMERZADA DBA NILARE DOLLAR ONLY IS GRANTED. FIRST, THE MOTION IS TIMELY UNDER CCP 473.5. SECOND, DEFENDANT AMERZADA'S DECLARATION DEMONSTRATES HIS LACK OF ACTUAL NOTICE IN TIME TO DEFEND THE ACTION. THIRD, DEFENDANT PROVIDES A PROPOSED ANSWER. FOURTH, THERE IS A POLICY TO HEAR CASES ON THE MERITS. DEFENDANT'S PROOF OF SERVICE INDICATES TIMELY SERVICE OF THE MOTION AND PLAINTIFF HAS ADDRESSED THE MERITS OF THE MOTION. "IT IS WELL SETTLED THAT THE APPEARANCE OF A PARTY AT THE HEARING OF A MOTION AND HIS OR HER OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION ON ITS MERITS IS A WAIVER OF ANY DEFECTS OR IRREGULARITIES IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. THIS RULE APPLIES EVEN WHEN NO NOTICE WAS GIVEN AT ALL." CARLTON V. QUINT, (2000) 77 CAL.APP.4TH 690, 697. ORDER PENDING. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489 (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgement On Mohammed Arif Arman Dba Dailey Dollar
Off Calendar Jun-24-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ernesto Sanchez, of Liberation Law Group, P.C., Tel. 415.695.1000, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MOHAMMED ARMAN'S Motion To Vacate Judgement On Mohammed Arif Arman dba Dailey Dollar is denied. Defendant Arman fails to demonstrate that he actually served the motion. Moreover, as to the merits, defendant fails to demonstrate just cause for relief. There is no declaration from counsel indicating that he did not have knowledge of the trial date. Any error of counsel is imputed to defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgement On Hanif Amerzada Dba Nilare Dollar Only
Off Calendar Jun-24-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ernesto Sanchez, of Liberation Law Group, P.C., Tel. 415.695.1000, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT HANIF AMERZADA'S Motion To Vacate Judgement On Hanif Amerzada dba Nilare Dollar Only is granted. First, the motion is timely under CCP 473.5. Second, defendant Amerzada's declaration demonstrates his lack of actual notice in time to defend the action. Third, defendant provides a proposed answer. Fourth, there is a policy to hear cases on the merits. Defendant's proof of service indicates timely service of the motion and plaintiff has addressed the merits of the motion. "It is well settled that the appearance of a party at the hearing of a motion and his or her opposition to the motion on its merits is a waiver of any defects or irregularities in the notice of motion. This rule applies even when no notice was given at all." Carlton v. Quint, (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697. Order pending. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
